item.title: Stress Test - Diminishing Returns Rule in Photography
item.description: Stress Test: Diminishing Returns Rule in Photography Knowledge Base Seed Rule Being Tested Rule: Use diminishing returns testing before adding notes to existing topics — skip or defer if the note fails 2+ of: utility (improves vault answers), connection (links to 2+ existing naturally), uniqueness (...

item.title: Seed Stress Test - Finance and Investment Knowledge Base
item.description: Seed Stress Test: Finance and Investment Knowledge Base Testing Seed rules against the finance and investment domain reveals unique challenges for managing wealth-building knowledge.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Conversational Knowledge Capture
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Conversational Knowledge Capture Knowledge from conversations, interviews, and verbal exchanges has properties distinct from written sources - context, relationship, and non-verbal cues that affect meaning but are hard to capture.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Building Knowledge Bases in Unfamiliar Domains
item.description: Frontier Exploration: Building Knowledge Bases in Unfamiliar Domains What to do when the AI doesn’t understand the topic it’s helping to document The Problem The Seed assumes an AI agent can: Understand the domain it’s working in Identify what constitutes quality knowledge Recognize when knowledge i...

item.title: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed Drop this file into any vault to bootstrap a healthy, self-improving knowledge base.

item.title: Atomic Note Principle
item.description: Atomic Note Principle A note must be atomic — it contains exactly one single idea.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Adaptation and Transcoding Knowledge
item.description: Note: The rules proposed in this note have been incorporated into the Seed (see Seed sections 6-7 under “Adaptation Knowledge”).

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Ephemeral and Event-Based Knowledge
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Ephemeral and Event-Based Knowledge The Problem The Seed has rules for many temporal dimensions of knowledge: Time-sensitive data (stock prices, weather) Sequential knowledge (history, recipes) Predictive knowledge (forecasts, projections) Living documents (continuously evolvi...

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Jargon vs Plain Language
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Jargon vs Plain Language in Knowledge Bases This note elaborates on Seed rules about terminology and multi-audience vaults.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Attention Signals
item.description: Frontier Exploration: Knowledge Attention Signals How should a knowledge base signal which notes need immediate attention without requiring manual tracking? The Problem The Seed has comprehensive lifecycle tracking (draft → active → evergreen → deprecated → archived) and review date tracking (last-r...
